# Feature: Interactive Mode Prompt for /forge:init

> Feature ID: FEAT-004
> Status: 🔧 implemented
> Created: 2026-04-17T00:00:00Z

## Description

Replace flag-only fast-mode discovery with an interactive prompt that surfaces fast vs. full mode by default. Mode-specific pre-flight tables, --full and --fast escape hatches, conflict detection, and resume-time mode switching with skipped-phase handling.

## Requirements

- Mode prompt rendered after resume detection and before pre-flight; default = Full
- Mode-specific pre-flight tables (Full = 12 phases; Fast = 7 [runs]/[skeleton]/[stubs] + 5 [deferred])
- --fast and --full flags suppress the prompt; --fast --full halts with conflict error
- Mode persisted to .forge/init-progress.json pre-Phase-1 and propagated into .forge/config.json by Phase 1
- Resume sub-prompt offers stored mode as default with switch option; switch warnings emitted (Fast→Full regenerates skipped, Full→Fast retains artifacts)
- Resume into a skipped phase emits △ advance message and jumps to the next active phase per fast-mode phase map (1, 2, 3, 7, 9, 10, 11, 12)
